page.title: Family Boat Day from Cabopino: Marbella Yacht Charter for All Ages

data.meta_description: Plan a Marbella family boat day from Cabopino with our yacht charter for all ages. From €749/2h, includes skipper, drinks, and snacks. Book now!
data.summary: Discover the perfect family boat day from Cabopino with Marbella yacht charter options for all ages. Our fleet includes child-friendly boats from €749/2h, with licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Family-friendly boats depart from Puerto Banús, just 10 minutes from Cabopino
Child-friendly charter from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39)
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%)
Onboard drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, plus light snacks
Maximum capacity ranges from 5 to 12 guests depending on boat choice
Cabopino beach and dunes are a popular stop for swimming and sand play
Ideal for multi-generational groups with calm waters along the Costa del Sol

faq.q: What is the best boat for a family with young children from Cabopino?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent choices. Both offer stable rides, shaded areas, and cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks. They depart from Puerto Banús, a short drive from Cabopino.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks on the boat?
faq.a: Our charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food, but glass bottles are not allowed for safety. We recommend packing sandwiches or fruit for the kids.

faq.q: Is swimming safe for children during the boat trip?
faq.a: Yes, swimming is safe in calm coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. Our licensed skipper will anchor in shallow, protected spots. Life jackets are available on board for all ages. Always supervise children near the water.

faq.q: How far is Cabopino from Puerto Banús departure point?
faq.a: Cabopino is about 8 km east of Puerto Banús, a 10-minute drive by car. Our boats depart from Puerto Banús marina, with free parking nearby. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early for boarding.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for children on a boat charter?
faq.a: There is no minimum age, but infants must be included in the passenger count. Life jackets are provided for all sizes. For safety, children under 5 should wear a life jacket at all times on deck.

faq.q: Can we book a half-day trip from Cabopino for a family?
faq.a: Absolutely. Half-day trips (4 hours) are available on request for most boats. The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 can be booked for 4 hours at a custom rate. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact pricing and availability.

faq.q: Are there any restrictions for children on the Mangusta 80?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is suitable for families, but requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719, which includes a free jet ski. Children must be supervised near the jet ski. Life jackets are provided for all ages.

faq.q: What should we bring for a family boat day from Cabopino?
faq.a: Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+), hats, sunglasses, towels, swimwear, and a light jacket for wind. For kids, pack extra snacks, a change of clothes, and any favourite toys. We provide all drinks and light snacks onboard.

data.body_html:
Planning a Marbella family boat day from Cabopino is the perfect way to enjoy the Costa del Sol with kids, grandparents, and everyone in between. With calm Mediterranean waters, child-friendly boats, and all-inclusive pricing from €749 for 2 hours, you can relax while the crew takes care of everything. Departing from Puerto Banús, just 10 minutes from Cabopino, our yacht charter for all ages ensures a stress-free day on the water.

Why Choose Cabopino for a Family Boat Day?

Cabopino is one of Marbella's best-kept secrets for families. Its sandy beach and protected dunes offer a natural playground, while the nearby marina provides easy access to our fleet. A Marbella family boat day from Cabopino lets you explore coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where kids can swim in shallow, clear water. The short transfer from Cabopino to Puerto Banús means more time on the boat and less time travelling.

Our Family-Friendly Fleet

We offer boats that suit families of all sizes. For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal. Both are stable, have shaded cockpits, and cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours – all inclusive. For larger families, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers more space and a free jet ski, starting at €4,719 for 4 hours. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, drinks, and snacks.

What’s Included in Your Family Charter

No hidden costs. Every booking includes:

  • Licensed skipper who knows the best family spots
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment, including life jackets for children
  • Spanish IVA (21% tax)

Just bring your swimwear and sunscreen. We handle the rest.

Best Itinerary for a Family Day on the Water

A typical Marbella family boat day from Cabopino starts at Puerto Banús. Head east towards Cabopino beach, then anchor at Cala del Faro for a swim. Continue to Cala Cortés for snorkelling, then cruise back past the Golden Mile and La Concha mountain views. If time allows, visit Estepona or Sotogrande for lunch. Your skipper will adjust the route based on sea conditions and your family’s preferences.

Safety Tips for Kids on a Boat Charter

Safety comes first. All our boats carry life jackets for children of all sizes. The skipper will brief you on safety rules before departure. Keep kids seated while the boat is moving, especially in open areas. Avoid glass bottles – use plastic cups provided. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but always follow the skipper’s advice.

How to Book Your Family Boat Day from Cabopino

Booking is easy. Choose your boat from our fleet, then contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, you can book directly online. For other boats, we’ll provide a custom quote. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer. Explore our full range of Marbella yacht charters for more options.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for a family with young children from Cabopino?

The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent choices. Both offer stable rides, shaded areas, and cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks. They depart from Puerto Banús, a short drive from Cabopino.

Can we bring our own food and drinks on the boat?

Our charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food, but glass bottles are not allowed for safety. We recommend packing sandwiches or fruit for the kids.

Is swimming safe for children during the boat trip?

Yes, swimming is safe in calm coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. Our licensed skipper will anchor in shallow, protected spots. Life jackets are available on board for all ages. Always supervise children near the water.

How far is Cabopino from Puerto Banús departure point?

Cabopino is about 8 km east of Puerto Banús, a 10-minute drive by car. Our boats depart from Puerto Banús marina, with free parking nearby. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early for boarding.

What is the minimum age for children on a boat charter?

There is no minimum age, but infants must be included in the passenger count. Life jackets are provided for all sizes. For safety, children under 5 should wear a life jacket at all times on deck.

Can we book a half-day trip from Cabopino for a family?

Absolutely. Half-day trips (4 hours) are available on request for most boats. The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 can be booked for 4 hours at a custom rate. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact pricing and availability.

Are there any restrictions for children on the Mangusta 80?

The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is suitable for families, but requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719, which includes a free jet ski. Children must be supervised near the jet ski. Life jackets are provided for all ages.

What should we bring for a family boat day from Cabopino?

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+), hats, sunglasses, towels, swimwear, and a light jacket for wind. For kids, pack extra snacks, a change of clothes, and any favourite toys. We provide all drinks and light snacks onboard.

For more family-friendly options, browse our Marbella yacht charters or read more about family boat days from Cabopino. Book your next adventure with us and create lasting memories on the water.
